meta.note: Curated overlay upgrading the GIRAI-only Italy entry to a curated node. Reuses atlas.json grammar. Every load-bearing claim carries source_ids + epistemic_level.

node.name: Italy
node.notes: First comprehensive national AI law in the EU (L.132/2025), but the substantive engine is the EU AI Act; the national law is governance scaffolding + a few high-temperature triggers. Supervisors are government agencies, not independent authorities. Band codes (S/IN) are INFERRED — confirm against Band Rubric v1.1.

claims.claim_text: Law 23 Sept 2025 no.132 — first comprehensive national AI law in the EU; approved 17 Sep 2025, in force 10 Oct 2025 (GU Serie Generale n.223, 25/09/2025).

Art.20 designates AgID as notifying authority and ACN as market-surveillance authority + single point of contact with the EU; sectoral powers of Banca d'Italia, CONSOB, IVASS, Garante, AGCOM preserved.
Art.26(1)(c) inserts Art.612-quater of the Criminal Code: 1–5 years for non-consensual dissemination of AI-falsified/altered images, video or audio causing unjust harm.
The law is explicitly subordinated to Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 and adds no obligations beyond the AI Act (Arts.1(2), 3(5)).
National supervisors are government agencies (AgID, ACN), not independent authorities; the choice was objected to by the European Commission opinion C(2024)7814 and by the Garante.
GIRAI 2024: overall 61.8/100, rank 7/138 globally (6th in Europe); weakest on Responsible AI Capacities (38.21).

Morphology = open gap: comprehensive statute on paper while realization lags — authorities only just designated, delegated decrees due by Oct 2026, and EU high-risk obligations deferred to 2 Dec 2027 by the Digital Omnibus.

readable.headline: Italy — first in the EU to legislate, but the engine is Brussels
readable.summary: Italy passed the EU's first comprehensive national AI law (132/2025), in force since 10 October 2025. But it deliberately adds no obligations beyond the EU AI Act: it is governance scaffolding plus a few sharp triggers — a new deepfake crime, rules for health, work, public administration and copyright, and protections for minors. The real regulatory engine remains the EU AI Act.
readable.how_it_works: The law designates two government agencies — AgID (notification) and ACN (market surveillance, EU contact point) — and delegates the substance to government decrees due by October 2026. EU high-risk obligations themselves were deferred to December 2027 by the Digital Omnibus, so on the ground the apparatus is named but not yet biting.
readable.technical_detail: Mixed gaze: ex-ante for conformity/notification (delegated to the EU regime via AgID) and for sectoral disclosure duties (Arts.4,11,13,14); ex-post for the new criminal offence (Art.612-quater c.p., 1–5 yrs). Broad scope (all AI/GPAI as defined by the EU AI Act, no national risk taxonomy). Medium institutional transparency (authorities are government agencies, not independent). Limited reciprocity (information rights for workers/clients; no horizontal right to contest beyond AI Act + GDPR Art.22).

to_verify: Verbatim text of Art.612-quater c.p. as inserted by Art.26(1)(c) — pull from the Normattiva consolidated text before publishing the page.
Primary link to European Commission opinion C(2024)7814 (TRIS procedure) — currently attested via law-firm sources only.
Italy's individual signature/ratification of the Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI (CETS No.225) — bound via EU signature; national status unconfirmed.
Formal adoption date of the Digital Omnibus on AI in the EU Official Journal (political agreement 7 May 2026; not yet adopted at compile time).
